full_context: Calculate the design current of a three‑phase circuit, which is the basis for voltage drop calculation.

variable_definitions.name: Design Current
variable_definitions.meaning: RMS current flowing in the circuit

variable_definitions.name: Design Active Power
variable_definitions.meaning: Active power transmitted by the circuit

variable_definitions.name: Line Voltage
variable_definitions.meaning: System line voltage

variable_definitions.name: Power Factor
variable_definitions.meaning: Power factor of the load

potential_questions: Given P_js=8kW, U=380V, cosφ=0.9, calculate I
task_description: 1. Obtain design active power P_js (convert to W); 2. Obtain line voltage U; 3. Obtain power factor cosφ; 4. Calculate √3; 5. Calculate I = P_js / (√3 × U × cosφ); 6. Output I

full_context: Calculate the area of a rectangular room, which is the basis for lighting calculation.

variable_definitions.name: Room Area
variable_definitions.meaning: Area of the zone to be illuminated

variable_definitions.name: Room Length
variable_definitions.meaning: Length of the room

variable_definitions.name: Room Width
variable_definitions.meaning: Width of the room

potential_questions: Given L=15m, W=10m, calculate A
task_description: 1. Obtain room length L; 2. Obtain room width W; 3. Calculate A = L × W; 4. Output A

full_context: Calculate the corrected open‑circuit voltage of a PV module at low temperature, which is used to determine the number of series modules.

variable_definitions.name: Corrected Open‑Circuit Voltage
variable_definitions.meaning: Module open‑circuit voltage considering temperature effect

variable_definitions.name: Nominal Open‑Circuit Voltage
variable_definitions.meaning: Module open‑circuit voltage at STC (25℃)

variable_definitions.name: Ambient Temperature
variable_definitions.meaning: Actual operating temperature of the module

variable_definitions.name: Temperature Coefficient of Voc
variable_definitions.meaning: Coefficient describing Voc variation with temperature

potential_questions: Given V_oc=49.5V, t=-15℃, K_v=-0.003/℃, calculate V_oc_corr
task_description: 1. Obtain nominal open‑circuit voltage V_oc; 2. Obtain ambient temperature t; 3. Obtain temperature coefficient K_v; 4. Calculate (t‑25); 5. Calculate V_oc_corr = V_oc × [1 + (t‑25)×K_v]; 6. Output V_oc_corr

full_context: Calculate the required number of battery strings based on total energy demand, system voltage, and available capacity per string, considering discharge efficiency η_bat.

variable_definitions.name: Number of Battery Strings
variable_definitions.meaning: Minimum number of battery strings to meet capacity requirement

variable_definitions.name: Required Total Capacity
variable_definitions.meaning: Total energy demand from previous calculation

variable_definitions.name: System DC Voltage
variable_definitions.meaning: Operating voltage of the battery bank

variable_definitions.name: Rated Capacity per String
variable_definitions.meaning: Capacity of one battery string at rated voltage

variable_definitions.name: Battery Discharge Efficiency
variable_definitions.meaning: Ratio of actual usable capacity to rated capacity

potential_questions: Given C_s=20kW·h, U_dc=48V, C_bat=100Ah, η_bat=0.9, calculate M
task_description: 1. Obtain required total capacity C_s (kW·h); 2. Obtain system voltage U_dc; 3. Obtain capacity per string C_bat; 4. Determine battery discharge efficiency η_bat; 5. Calculate usable energy per string = (U_dc × C_bat × η_bat) / 1000 (kW·h); 6. Calculate M = C_s / usable energy per string, round up; 7. Output M

full_context: Calculate annual power generation revenue based on annual output and on‑grid tariff, in ten‑thousand yuan.

variable_definitions.name: Annual Generation Revenue
variable_definitions.meaning: Revenue from annual on‑grid power generation

variable_definitions.name: Annual On‑Grid Energy
variable_definitions.meaning: Annual on‑grid energy from previous calculation

variable_definitions.name: On‑Grid Tariff
variable_definitions.meaning: Power purchase price from the grid

potential_questions: Given E_p=90.48 GW·h, Price=0.4 yuan/kWh, calculate R
task_description: 1. Obtain annual on‑grid energy E_p (convert to kWh: ×10⁶); 2. Obtain on‑grid tariff Price; 3. Calculate revenue (yuan) = E_p(kWh) × Price; 4. Convert to 10k yuan (divide by 10000); 5. Output R

full_context: Judge whether the actual deformation of a structure or member is less than or equal to the maximum allowable deformation specified by the code.

variable_definitions.name: Calculated Deflection
variable_definitions.meaning: Actual deformation obtained from mechanical calculation

variable_definitions.name: Allowable Maximum Deflection
variable_definitions.meaning: Deformation limit specified by the code

potential_questions: Given f_calc=10mm, [f_max]=8.75mm, determine if satisfied
task_description: 1. Calculate allowable maximum deflection [f_max]; 2. Obtain actual calculated deflection f_calc; 3. Compare f_calc and [f_max]; 4. Output result (Yes/No)

full_context: Judge whether the minimum measured strength of concrete specimens meets the code requirement (e.g., not less than 0.85 times the design strength).

variable_definitions.name: Minimum Measured Strength
variable_definitions.meaning: Minimum compressive strength among all specimens

variable_definitions.name: Design Strength Grade
variable_definitions.meaning: Strength corresponding to concrete design grade

variable_definitions.name: Minimum Strength Factor
variable_definitions.meaning: Ratio of minimum required strength to design strength

potential_questions: Given X_min=21.5MPa, R=25MPa, k=0.85, determine if satisfied
task_description: 1. Obtain minimum measured strength X_min; 2. Obtain design strength R; 3. Determine code‑specified minimum strength factor k; 4. Calculate allowable minimum strength = k × R; 5. Compare X_min and allowable minimum strength; 6. Output result (Yes/No)

full_context: Calculate the minimum additional anti‑sliding force required to meet the code‑specified safety factor when current resistance is insufficient.

variable_definitions.name: Required Additional Anti‑Sliding Force
variable_definitions.meaning: Anti‑sliding force to be added for satisfactory safety factor

variable_definitions.name: Required Safety Factor
variable_definitions.meaning: Minimum safety factor specified by the code

variable_definitions.name: Total Sliding Force
variable_definitions.meaning: Sum of forces driving slope failure

variable_definitions.name: Total Current Anti‑Sliding Force
variable_definitions.meaning: Sum of current resisting forces

potential_questions: Given S=950kN, R=1425kN, [K]=1.20, calculate ΔR
task_description: 1. Calculate current safety factor K = R / S; 2. Compare K and [K]; 3. If not satisfied, calculate required total resistance R_req = [K] × S; 4. Calculate required additional force ΔR = R_req - R; 5. Output ΔR

full_context: Calculate atmospheric correction factor A used in corona loss calculation.

variable_definitions.name: Atmospheric Correction Factor
variable_definitions.meaning: Parameter correcting air density due to pressure and temperature

variable_definitions.name: Atmospheric Pressure
variable_definitions.meaning: Actual atmospheric pressure at measurement site

variable_definitions.name: Air Temperature
variable_definitions.meaning: Ambient air temperature at measurement site

potential_questions: Given p=101.3kPa, θ=20℃, calculate A
task_description: 1. Obtain atmospheric pressure p; 2. Obtain air temperature θ; 3. Calculate 273+θ; 4. Calculate A = 2.94p / (273+θ); 5. Output A

full_context: Calculate the required number of luminaires based on room dimensions, target illuminance, and luminaire parameters.

variable_definitions.name: Number of Luminaires
variable_definitions.meaning: Minimum number of luminaires to meet illuminance requirement

variable_definitions.name: Target Average Illuminance
variable_definitions.meaning: Required average illuminance on the working plane

variable_definitions.name: Room Length
variable_definitions.meaning: Length of the room

variable_definitions.name: Room Width
variable_definitions.meaning: Width of the room

variable_definitions.name: Luminous Flux per Luminaire
variable_definitions.meaning: Light output of one luminaire

variable_definitions.name: Utilization Factor
variable_definitions.meaning: Ratio of flux reaching the working plane

variable_definitions.name: Maintenance Factor
variable_definitions.meaning: Reduction factor considering lamp aging and dirt

potential_questions: Given E_c=300lx, L=15m, W=10m, Φ=5000lm, U=0.65, K=0.8, calculate N
task_description: 1. Obtain target illuminance E_c; 2. Obtain room dimensions L and W, calculate area A=L×W; 3. Obtain luminous flux Φ; 4. Obtain utilization factor U and maintenance factor K; 5. Calculate N = (E_c × A) / (Φ × U × K); 6. Round up the result; 7. Output N

full_context: Calculate the maximum number of series PV modules considering inverter voltage limit and low‑temperature behavior.

variable_definitions.name: Maximum Series Count
variable_definitions.meaning: Maximum series modules without overvoltage at low temperature

variable_definitions.name: Inverter Max Input Voltage
variable_definitions.meaning: Upper limit of DC input voltage allowed by inverter

variable_definitions.name: Nominal Module Voc
variable_definitions.meaning: Module open‑circuit voltage at STC

variable_definitions.name: Minimum Ambient Temperature
variable_definitions.meaning: Lowest operating temperature of the module

variable_definitions.name: Temperature Coefficient of Voc
variable_definitions.meaning: Coefficient describing Voc variation with temperature

potential_questions: Given V_dcmax=1500V, V_oc=49.5V, t=-15℃, K_v=-0.003/℃, calculate N_max
task_description: 1. Obtain inverter max input voltage V_dcmax; 2. Obtain nominal module Voc V_oc; 3. Obtain minimum temperature t and coefficient K_v; 4. Calculate temperature correction factor [1 + (t‑25)×K_v]; 5. Calculate corrected Voc = V_oc × factor; 6. Calculate N_max = V_dcmax / corrected Voc, round down; 7. Output N_max

full_context: Calculate the synchronous speed of a three‑phase induction motor.

variable_definitions.name: Synchronous Speed
variable_definitions.meaning: Speed of the rotating magnetic field

variable_definitions.name: Power Frequency
variable_definitions.meaning: Grid AC frequency

variable_definitions.name: Number of Pole Pairs
variable_definitions.meaning: Number of magnetic pole pairs of the motor

potential_questions: Given f=50Hz, p=4, calculate n_s
task_description: 1. Obtain power frequency f; 2. Obtain number of pole pairs p; 3. Calculate n_s = 60f / p; 4. Output n_s.

full_context: Calculate the slip ratio of an induction motor.

variable_definitions.name: Slip Ratio
variable_definitions.meaning: Ratio of speed difference to synchronous speed

variable_definitions.name: Synchronous Speed
variable_definitions.meaning: Synchronous speed from previous calculation

variable_definitions.name: Actual Speed
variable_definitions.meaning: Actual operating speed of the rotor

potential_questions: Given n_s=1500 r/min, n=1450 r/min, calculate s
task_description: 1. Obtain synchronous speed n_s; 2. Obtain actual speed n; 3. Calculate (n_s - n); 4. Calculate s = [(n_s - n) / n_s] × 100%; 5. Output s.

full_context: Calculate busbar short‑circuit capacity to evaluate system strength and voltage support capability.

variable_definitions.name: Short‑Circuit Capacity
variable_definitions.meaning: Apparent power available at busbar during short circuit

variable_definitions.name: Average Voltage
variable_definitions.meaning: Operating voltage of the busbar

variable_definitions.name: Short‑Circuit Current
variable_definitions.meaning: Three‑phase short‑circuit current from previous calculation

potential_questions: Given U_av=10.2kV, I_k''=7.36kA, calculate S_d
task_description: 1. Obtain busbar average voltage U_av; 2. Obtain three‑phase short‑circuit current I_k''; 3. Calculate √3; 4. Calculate S_d = √3 × U_av × I_k''; 5. Output S_d.

full_context: Calculate the rated line current of a three‑phase shunt capacitor bank.

variable_definitions.name: Capacitor Rated Current
variable_definitions.meaning: Line current of capacitor bank at rated voltage and power

variable_definitions.name: Compensation Capacity
variable_definitions.meaning: Reactive compensation capacity from previous calculation

variable_definitions.name: Rated Voltage
variable_definitions.meaning: Rated line voltage of the capacitor bank

potential_questions: Given Q_c=400kvar, U_n=0.4kV, calculate I_c
task_description: 1. Obtain compensation capacity Q_c; 2. Obtain rated voltage U_n; 3. Calculate √3; 4. Calculate I_c = Q_c / (√3 × U_n) (unify units); 5. Output I_c.

full_context: Estimate the total grounding resistance of N identical grounding rods in parallel.

variable_definitions.name: Total Grounding Resistance
variable_definitions.meaning: Equivalent grounding resistance of N rods in parallel

variable_definitions.name: Single Grounding Resistance
variable_definitions.meaning: Grounding resistance of one rod from previous calculation

variable_definitions.name: Number of Grounding Rods
variable_definitions.meaning: Number of parallel rods

potential_questions: Given R_E=25Ω, require R_total<10Ω, find minimum integer N
task_description: 1. Calculate single grounding resistance R_E; 2. Obtain target total resistance R_target; 3. Estimate N ≥ R_E / R_target; 4. Round up the result; 5. Output N.

full_context: Select a protective fuse for a circuit or device; its rated current shall not be less than design current multiplied by reliability factor.

variable_definitions.name: Fuse Rated Current
variable_definitions.meaning: Current that can pass continuously without blowing

variable_definitions.name: Reliability Factor
variable_definitions.meaning: Factor considering starting current, tolerance, etc.

variable_definitions.name: Design Current
variable_definitions.meaning: Operating current from previous calculation

potential_questions: Given I_b=40A, K_k=1.5, find minimum I_fu
task_description: 1. Calculate design current I_b; 2. Obtain reliability factor K_k; 3. Calculate I_fu_min = K_k × I_b; 4. Select standard value ≥ I_fu_min; 5. Output I_fu (minimum).

full_context: Verify that the total thermal resistance of the transistor heat‑transfer path is ≤ maximum allowable thermal resistance.

variable_definitions.name: Required Maximum Thermal Resistance
variable_definitions.meaning: Maximum allowable junction‑to‑ambient thermal resistance

variable_definitions.name: Junction‑to‑Case Thermal Resistance
variable_definitions.meaning: Internal thermal resistance from datasheet

variable_definitions.name: Contact Thermal Resistance
variable_definitions.meaning: Thermal resistance between case and heatsink

variable_definitions.name: Heatsink Thermal Resistance
variable_definitions.meaning: Thermal resistance from heatsink to ambient

potential_questions: Given R_th_required=7.33 K/W, R_th_jc=1.5 K/W, R_th_cs=0.5 K/W, R_th_sa=2.0 K/W, verify if satisfied
task_description: 1. Calculate required max thermal resistance R_th_required; 2. Obtain R_th_jc; 3. Obtain R_th_cs and R_th_sa; 4. Calculate actual total resistance R_th_actual = sum; 5. Compare R_th_actual and R_th_required; 6. Output result (Yes/No).

formula: V_{out}' = V_{in} \frac{R_2 // R_L}{R_1 + (R_2 // R_L)}, where R_2 // R_L = \frac{R_2 R_L}{R_2 + R_L}
full_context: Calculate the output voltage of a voltage divider considering the loading effect.

variable_definitions.name: Loaded Output Voltage
variable_definitions.meaning: Actual output voltage with load connected

variable_definitions.name: Input Voltage
variable_definitions.meaning: Input voltage of the divider

variable_definitions.name: Upper Arm Resistor
variable_definitions.meaning: Resistor from previous calculation

variable_definitions.name: Lower Arm Resistor
variable_definitions.meaning: Lower resistor of the divider

variable_definitions.name: Load Resistance
variable_definitions.meaning: Equivalent load at the output

potential_questions: Given V_in=12V, R1=9kΩ, R2=3kΩ, R_L=10kΩ, calculate V_out'
task_description: 1. Calculate upper resistor R1; 2. Calculate parallel value R_parallel = R2//RL; 3. Calculate total resistance R_total = R1 + R_parallel; 4. Calculate V_out' = V_in × (R_parallel / R_total); 5. Output V_out'.

formula: V_c(t) = V_{cc} (1 - e^{-t/\tau}), solve for t when V_c(t)=V_{target}
full_context: Calculate the time required for capacitor voltage to rise from 0 to target voltage in an RC charging circuit.

variable_definitions.name: Charging Time
variable_definitions.meaning: Time to reach target voltage from 0

variable_definitions.name: Supply Voltage
variable_definitions.meaning: DC voltage of the RC circuit

variable_definitions.name: Target Voltage
variable_definitions.meaning: Target capacitor voltage

variable_definitions.name: Time Constant
variable_definitions.meaning: RC time constant from previous calculation

potential_questions: Given V_cc=5V, V_target=3.15V, τ=1s, calculate t
task_description: 1. Calculate time constant τ; 2. Obtain V_cc and V_target; 3. Derive t = -τ × ln(1 - V_target/V_cc); 4. Substitute values and compute t; 5. Output t. (Note: t≈τ when V_target=0.63V_cc)

full_context: Calculate the impedance of a lossy series LC circuit at resonant frequency.

variable_definitions.name: Resonant Impedance
variable_definitions.meaning: Total impedance magnitude of series LC at resonance

variable_definitions.name: Series Resistance
variable_definitions.meaning: Total equivalent series resistance in the loop

potential_questions: Given R_s=50Ω, calculate Z_res
task_description: 1. Calculate required resonant capacitance C; 2. Obtain total series resistance R_s; 3. At resonance, inductive and capacitive reactances cancel; Z_res = R_s; 4. Output Z_res.

full_context: Estimate the maximum allowable collector current of a transistor based on power dissipation and V_CE.

variable_definitions.name: Maximum Allowable Collector Current
variable_definitions.meaning: Current limit at given V_CE without exceeding power rating

variable_definitions.name: Power Dissipation
variable_definitions.meaning: Maximum allowable power dissipation of the transistor

variable_definitions.name: Collector‑Emitter Voltage
variable_definitions.meaning: Voltage between collector and emitter

potential_questions: Given P=0.5W, V_CE=10V, calculate I_C_max
task_description: 1. Calculate static collector current I_C; 2. Obtain max power P and voltage V_CE; 3. Estimate I_C_max ≈ P / V_CE; 4. Compare I_C and I_C_max for safety; 5. Output I_C_max and judgment.

full_context: Calculate electricity cost based on power, operating time, and electricity tariff.

variable_definitions.name: Electricity Cost
variable_definitions.meaning: Cost of energy consumed by equipment

variable_definitions.name: Input Power
variable_definitions.meaning: Active power drawn from the grid

variable_definitions.name: Operating Time
variable_definitions.meaning: Daily or monthly operating hours

variable_definitions.name: Electricity Tariff
variable_definitions.meaning: Price per kilowatt‑hour

potential_questions: Given P_in=55kW, t=10h, Price=0.8 yuan/kWh, calculate Cost
task_description: 1. Calculate motor efficiency η; 2. Obtain input power P_in, time t, tariff Price; 3. Calculate energy consumption = P_in × t (kWh); 4. Calculate Cost = energy × Price; 5. Output Cost.
